title: C Introduction
content:
### What is C?

C is a general-purpose, procedural programming language developed by Dennis Ritchie at Bell Labs in 1972. It is one of the most influential programming languages and forms the foundation for many modern languages like C++, Java, C#, and Python.

### History of C

C was created to develop the UNIX operating system. It evolved from an earlier language called B, which was based on BCPL. The language was standardized by ANSI in 1989 (C89/C90) and later by ISO in 1999 (C99), 2011 (C11), and 2018 (C17).

---

### Why Learn C?

| Feature | Benefit |
|---------|--------|
| Speed | Compiled language with excellent performance |
| Portability | Code can run on virtually any platform |
| Low-level access | Direct memory manipulation via pointers |
| System programming | Used for OS kernels, embedded systems, drivers |
| Foundation | Teaches core programming concepts deeply |

### Common Uses of C

- Operating systems (Linux, Windows kernel components)
- Embedded systems and microcontrollers
- Game engines and graphics libraries
- Compilers and interpreters
- Database systems (MySQL, SQLite)

---

> **Key Point:** C gives you fine-grained control over hardware and memory, making it ideal for systems programming where performance and efficiency are critical.
